passing_score_scale: Pass/fail; scaled scores established by NBEO
question_types: Objective Structured Clinical Examination — OSCE (Part III)
exam_format: Written (Parts I, II, TMOD) and OSCE (Part III) at NBEO-designated sites

testing_centers.notes: All NBEO examinations are administered at NBEO-designated testing sites. No online proctoring is available. Scheduling information available at optometry.org.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match registration exactly.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, or reference materials permitted. Part I and Part II are traditional 2-day written examinations. Part III is an objective structured clinical examination (OSCE) with standardized patients. TMOD is required in states that mandate a separate therapeutic pharmaceutical licensure exam. Renewal and maintenance requirements are determined by individual state boards of optometry.
renewal_options: Continuing education and renewal requirements vary by state board of optometry. Candidates should verify requirements with their state licensing authority.

retake_policy.notes: Retake policies are defined by NBEO; refer to optometry.org for current waiting periods and attempt limits per examination part.

official_study_resources.title: NBEO Examination Information
official_study_resources.notes: Official exam specifications and candidate information at optometry.org
